channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: No. 2 Oregon routs Purdue 35-0, remains on top of Big Ten with first road shutout in over 30 years
item.description: WEST LAFAYETTE, Ind. (AP) — Oregon coach Dan Lanning challenged his team to see how it would respond to a long road trip on a short week following its 32-31 victory over then-No. 2 Ohio State last week. The No. 2 Ducks aced that test Friday night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Yankees get to Guardians closer Clase again, score 2 runs in 9th to win 8-6 and take 3-1 ALCS lead
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Cleveland All-Star closer Emmanuel Clase gave up two runs in the ninth inning and the New York Yankees moved closer to another World Series, beating the Guardians 8-6 on Friday night to take a 3-1 lead in the American League Championship Series.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dodgers' Jack Flaherty flops in 2nd NLCS outing vs. Mets, who force Game 6 in Los Angeles
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Instead of pitching his hometown team into the World Series, Jack Flaherty sent the NL Championship Series back to California. Dominant in the opener, Flaherty flopped in Game 5.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Taylor Pendrith avoids biggest wind and stays in front in Las Vegas with a long day ahead
item.description: Strong wind with gusts approaching 50 mph caused a four-hour delay in the Shriners Children's Open in Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Kadary Richmond talks expectations for senior season at St. John's
item.description: QUEENS, N.Y. (PIX11) – Kadary Richmond helped lead Seton Hall to an NIT Championship last season. He is now getting ready for his final year in the Big East, but this season he is suiting up to play for Rick Pitino at St. John’s. Watch the video player for more on this story.
item.dc:creator: Perry Sook, PIX 11

item.title: Mets extend season again, beating Dodgers 12-6 to send NLCS back to LA for Game 6
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Pete Alonso golfed an early three-run homer, and the New York Mets hammered an ineffective Jack Flaherty, extending the National League Championship Series with a 12-6 victory over the Los Angeles Dodgers in Game 5 on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Guardians starting Tanner Bibee in Game 5 against Yankees after short outing earlier in ALCS
item.description: Tanner Bibee is getting a second crack at the Yankees in the AL Championship Series. His first one didn't go so well. The right-hander will start for the Cleveland Guardians in Game 5 on Saturday after he was pulled from Game 2 in the second inning by manager Stephen Vogt.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 49ers rookie Ricky Pearsall will make his debut 50 days after being shot in the chest
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — San Francisco 49ers rookie receiver Ricky Pearsall will be activated and make his NFL debut Sunday against Kansas City 50 days after he was shot in the chest.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Temptations serenade Mets fans at NLCS as founder admits he roots for Dodgers
item.description: Sixty years after its debut, The Temptations’ “My Girl” has become a hit at Citi Field since New York Mets star Francisco Lindor began using it as his walk-up song in late May.
item.dc:creator: Ronald Blum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dallas Wings fire coach Latricia Trammell after 2 seasons
item.description: The Dallas Wings fired coach Latricia Trammell on Friday. Trammell went 31-49 in two seasons with the Dallas Wings, including 2-3 in playoff appearances. This past season the team went 9-31 and missed the WNBA postseason for the first time since 2020.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lynx force Game 5 of WNBA Finals on Carleton's free throws to beat Liberty 82-80
item.description: Bridget Carleton made two free throws with 2 seconds left, and the Minnesota Lynx forced a decisive Game 5 of the WNBA Finals, beating the New York Liberty 82-80.
item.dc:creator: Doug Feinberg, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tampa Bay Rowdies move to IMG Academy for final 2 regular-season games
item.description: The Tampa Bay Rowdies will play their final two regular-season matches at IMG Academy due to the impacts of Hurricane Milton and Helene.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Mets need their biggest comeback yet to reach the World Series
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets insist their greatest rebound of 2024 may be yet to come. “If we come back from this, then it’s going to be a heck of a story.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Broncos dominate Saints in Payton's return to New Orleans
item.description: For all four quarters. For all 60 minutes. The Denver Broncos were in complete control of the Saints.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: South Florida Athletics named a 'Best College Athletic Department' by Front Office Sports
item.description: TAMPA (USF) - University of South Florida Athletics has been named one of Front Office Sports’ “Best College Athletic Departments” for 2024.
item.dc:creator: Brian Siegrist, USF, WFLA, NBC

item.title: San Diego Wave FC gets new ownership after historic sale finalized
item.description: Seven months after it was first reported San Diego Wave FC would be getting new ownership, the National Women's Soccer League club announced on Thursday it was officially sold.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Criticism sparked over Snapdragon Stadium field conditions
item.description: The San Diego Wave FC lost 1-0 to Mexico’s Club America Wednesday. Both teams are advancing to the semifinals, but it was the turf that was the talk of the tailgate.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers lead Mets 2-1 ahead of NLCS Game 4
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — On a night of misplayed comebackers, the New York Mets put themselves in a need of yet another comeback. Luis Severino twice botched grounders, leading to a pair of unearned runs in the second inning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Shohei Ohtani homers as the Dodgers rout the Mets 8-0 for a 2-1 lead in the NLCS
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — By the time Shohei Ohtani stepped to the plate in the eighth inning Wednesday night, the Los Angeles Dodgers were already in control. Once the ball left his bat, Game 3 of the National League Championship Series was over.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Lonzo Ball makes long-awaited return from knee injuries, scores 10 in Bulls' preseason game
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — When the moment finally arrived, after multiple knee surgeries and one grueling rehab, Lonzo Ball raised his right arm and blew a kiss to the cheering United Center crowd. Fans rose to their feet as the announcement echoed through the arena: “Checking in for the Chicago Bulls! No.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Injured Alex Cobb dropped from Guardians' ALCS roster, replaced by Ben Lively
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Pitcher Alex Cobb was dropped from Cleveland's American League Championship Series roster on Tuesday after he got hurt yet again in the opener, leaving the Guardians in search of a Game 5 starter against the New York Yankees. “It’s been a challenging year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Caleb Williams and the Bears are looking good at their bye with 3 straight wins and a 4-2 mark
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Caleb Williams is settling into a rhythm, and the Bears are racking up wins. Overall, things are looking good for Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Grand Junction football's future looks bright
item.description: At 6-1, it’s the Tigers' best start to a season since 2011. And Grand Junction appears to be set for long-term success.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Los Angeles Rams implementing new AI technology during football games
item.description: The technologically advanced home of the Los Angeles Rams is about to have a new way for fans to track players’ statistics from their seats during the game.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Doubs catches 2 TD passes in his return from a suspension to help Packers rout Cardinals 34-13
item.description: Romeo Doubs wanted to make amends after serving a one-game suspension for what the Green Bay Packers described as conduct detrimental to the team.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former LSU pole vaulter Mondo Duplantis proposes to girlfriend during Vogue photo shoot
item.description: Duplantis got down on one knee and proposed to Desiré Inglander while taking photos in The Hamptons. According to Vogue Scandinavia, the couple has been together for four years.
item.dc:creator: Trinity Velazquez, NewsNation

item.title: North Carolina football player dies from rare lung cancer at 23
item.description: UNC had honored Tylee Craft, 23, during Saturday's game. Coach Mack Brown, however, said he hadn't learned of Craft's death until after the game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Bears vs. Jaguars: Teams battling in early-morning tilt at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in London
item.description: UPDATE: The Bears and Jaguars have announced their inactives for Sunday's game. Inactives for the Bears are DB Jaquan Brisker, DB Tyrique Stevenson, OL Nate Davis, DL Zacch Pickens, WR Velus Jones Jr., DB Terrell Smith and DL Dominque Robinson.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: North Carolina football player Tylee Craft dies from rare lung cancer at 23
item.description: North Carolina football player Tylee Craft died Saturday morning from a rare form of lung cancer, coach Mack Brown said. “This young man fought so hard for his two and a half years,” Brown said. “The doctors told us he outlived what he should’ve.
item.dc:creator: DOUG BONJOUR, Associated Press, Fox 5
